titleStmt.title: Letter from John R. Moorhead to Sir Matthew Nathan, 8 December, 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John R. Moorhead

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter from John R. Moorhead, Antrim and Belfast Crown solicitor to Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939), under-secretary for Ireland. In The King v Mrs. E. Morgan case, Mrs. Morgan has been tried for illegally drawing Army Separation Allowance, and is now required to make restitution. John encloses a cheque for £6. 10s, comprised of £5, which Mrs. Morgan produced while in court, and three installments of 10s paid since. A handwritten note at the bottom of the letter gives accounting instructions and states how future payments are to be dealt with.Sir Matthew Nathan was the under-secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers, CSO RP/1916/20780

correspAction.placeName: 30 Rosemary Street, Belfast, County Antrim, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Counties of Antrim & Belfast Crown Solicitor's Office. 30 Rosemary Street, Belfast. 8th. Decr. 1915
MOOREHEAD & WOOD,
Solicitors
JOHN R. MOOREHEAD,
Crown Solicitor.
Telephone No 605
THE UNDER SECRETARY/ SIR/ The King v Mrs. E. Morgan As directed by your Minute of the 6th. instant I
enclose herewith cheque in favour of The Regimental Paymaster,
Dublin for Â£6 .10. 0. being Â£5 handed into Court by Mrs. Morgan,
and three instalments of 10/- each paid by her to me since. There was no person nominated to receive the instalments
but the defendant has been paying same to me. The Judge directed
the Â£5 to be handed over to me. I am, Sir.
Your obedt. servant, John R Moorehead I Forward cheque to Regt
Paymaster Dublin with
copy War Office letter & say
that future payments will be
made direct by Mr Moorehead CJ
I agree with Mr Moorehead
9.12.15
